test_cases.description: NewClient returns a non-nil *Client with all default field values correctly set when called with a baseURL and no options.
preconditions: The apikit package is compiled and importable
test_cases.expected: Non-nil *Client; httpClient == http.DefaultClient; mountPoint == "/api/v1"; apiKey == ""; requestID == ""
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
client = NewClient("https://api.example.com")
assert client != nil
assert client.httpClient == http.DefaultClient
assert client.mountPoint == "/api/v1"
assert client.apiKey == ""
assert client.requestID == ""

test_cases.description: NewClient strips a trailing slash from the baseURL before storing it on the Client struct.
preconditions: The apikit package is compiled and importable
test_cases.expected: client.baseURL == "https://api.example.com" (no trailing slash)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
client = NewClient("https://api.example.com/")
assert client.baseURL == "https://api.example.com"

test_cases.description: Client is safe for concurrent use — multiple goroutines calling methods simultaneously produce no data races.
preconditions: A running httptest.Server returning valid JSON responses
The apikit package compiled with -race flag
test_cases.input: NewClient(server.URL, WithAPIKey("key")); 50 goroutines each calling client.Healthz(ctx) concurrently
test_cases.expected: All calls complete without data races detected by the Go race detector; all return valid responses or errors
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
server = httptest.NewServer(handler returning 200 HealthResponse)
defer server.Close()
client = NewClient(server.URL)
var wg sync.WaitGroup
for i in 0..49:
  wg.Add(1)
  go func():
    defer wg.Done()
    resp, err = client.Healthz(context.Background())
    assert err == nil OR err != nil  // any result is acceptable; no race
wg.Wait()
// Run with: go test -race ./...

test_cases.description: The do method passes the caller-supplied context.Context to the request, enabling cancellation.

  time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)

  w.Write(json({"status":"ok"}))
)

assert err != nil

property_tests.description: Client immutability guarantees concurrent safety: no field is modified after NewClient returns, making all concurrent method calls safe.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any Client c = NewClient(baseURL, opts...); any combination of 10-100 goroutines calling any subset of client methods simultaneously
property_tests.invariant_check: Run go test -race ./...; for any set of concurrent method calls on c, the race detector reports no data races. All goroutines observe the same apiKey, mountPoint, requestID, baseURL, and httpClient values set during construction.

property_tests.description: APIError classification is exhaustive: for any HTTP response with status code >= 400, do always returns a non-nil *APIError with Code set to the HTTP status code.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any HTTP status code S in range [400, 599]; response body may be valid JSON envelope, HTML, plain text, or empty
property_tests.invariant_check: For each generated (S, body) pair: call any SDK method against a mock server returning (S, body); assert errors.As(err, &apiErr) == true; assert apiErr.Code == S; assert apiErr != nil. The loop terminates after at most len(tested_status_codes) * len(body_types) iterations.

property_tests.description: Plain errors always wrap their underlying cause: errors.Unwrap returns non-nil for every plain error category returned by do.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any plain error scenario: network failure (connection refused), context cancellation, context deadline exceeded, JSON decode error on 2xx, marshal error
property_tests.invariant_check: For each error scenario: trigger the error condition; call errors.Unwrap(err); assert result != nil; assert errors.As(err, &apiErr) == false. Each scenario executes in bounded time (single request, no retry).

property_tests.description: Mount point normalization is idempotent: for any path string p, applying WithMountPoint(p) always produces a mountPoint starting with exactly one '/' and with no trailing '/'.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any string p from the set: "", "api", "/api", "api/", "/api/", "api/v1", "/api/v1", "/api/v1/", "//api", "/api//v1"
property_tests.invariant_check: For each p: c = NewClient("http://example.com", WithMountPoint(p)); assert c.mountPoint starts with exactly one '/'; assert c.mountPoint does not end with '/' (unless mountPoint == '/'). Applying WithMountPoint(c.mountPoint) again produces the same result (idempotent).

property_tests.description: Health probes always bypass mount point: regardless of mountPoint configuration, Healthz/Readyz/Version always use baseURL + path.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any Client c with any mountPoint value (default, custom, empty, deeply nested); any baseURL value
property_tests.invariant_check: For each (baseURL, mountPoint) pair: set up mock server; call Healthz(ctx), Readyz(ctx), Version(ctx); capture request paths; assert captured path == '/healthz', '/readyz', '/version' respectively; assert captured path does NOT contain mountPoint prefix (unless mountPoint == '/').

property_tests.description: List endpoints return non-nil empty slice for empty server response: for any list method, a server returning [] produces a non-nil Go slice of length 0.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any list endpoint method (ListKeys, ListTokens, ListUsers, ListOrgs, ListOrgMembers, ListUserKeys, ListUserTokens, ListUserOrgs, GetProviders); mock server returns []
property_tests.invariant_check: For each list method m: configure mock server to return 200 with body []; call m(ctx, ...); assert result != nil; assert len(result) == 0; assert err == nil. Bounded by number of list methods (constant).

property_tests.description: Authorization header is set iff apiKey is non-empty: for any request, the Authorization header is present with 'Bearer <key>' if and only if apiKey is non-empty.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any Client c with apiKey = any string value (empty or non-empty); any SDK method call
property_tests.invariant_check: For apiKey == "": captured request has no Authorization header. For apiKey == k (non-empty): captured request has Authorization == "Bearer " + k. Test at least 5 different non-empty key values and the empty string. No iteration is unbounded.

property_tests.description: ErrNotModified is returned iff the server responds with 304: for any conditional GET method, ErrNotModified appears if and only if the server status is 304, and the *Response[T] is always nil in that case.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any conditional GET method (GetUser, GetUserByID, GetToken, GetOrg); server response status is any value from {200, 304}
property_tests.invariant_check: For status 200: assert errors.Is(err, ErrNotModified) == false; assert resp != nil. For status 304: assert errors.Is(err, ErrNotModified) == true; assert resp == nil. Bounded by 4 methods * 2 status values = 8 test executions.

property_tests.description: UpdateUserRequest always includes full_name in JSON body: for any call to UpdateUser or UpdateUserByID with any FullName value, the JSON body always contains the 'full_name' key.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any string value for UpdateUserRequest.FullName, including empty string, whitespace-only, very long strings, and strings with special characters
property_tests.invariant_check: For each FullName value v: marshal &UpdateUserRequest{FullName: v}; assert JSON output contains key '"full_name"'; assert JSON output does not omit the key. Also capture server-side request body in integration test and assert 'full_name' key is always present.

property_tests.description: Canonical types are defined exactly once in the apikit package: each domain type name appears in exactly one Go file.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: All Go source files in the apikit package
property_tests.invariant_check: For each type name in [User, APIKeyMeta, APIKeyFull, PAT, PATFull, Organization, OAuthProvider, AuthCallbackRequest, AuthCallbackResponse, CreateUserRequest, UpdateUserRequest, CreateTokenRequest, CreateOrgRequest, UpdateOrgRequest, HealthResponse, VersionResponse, RevokeKeyResponse, ListUsersOptions, ListOrgsOptions]: grep for 'type <Name> struct' across all *.go files in package; count == 1. go build ./... succeeds with no redeclaration errors.

client = NewClient("http://localhost:19998", WithAPIKey("key"))
